steps.title: Identify the disagreement signal
steps.content: The word “But” at the start of the following sentence signals that Arnold is about to disagree with The Times' position.

steps.title: Apply the signal to identify the function
steps.content: Choice B correctly identifies the sentence's function: it presents The Times' position on individual freedom, establishing a viewpoint that Arnold opposes. The other three either misrepresent what the sentence claims (A), contradict its actual relationship to the author's view (C), or introduce ideas not in the passage (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
